definition.meaning: Enclosure, garden, field, yard; farm, manor; homestead, dwelling house, mansion; later group of houses, village
Inhabited place having some degree of local government; village, settlement

definition.cognates: Old Saxon tun, Old Norse tún "enclosure, homestead", Old Frisian tun "fence", Dutch tuin "garden", Old High German zūn, German Zaun "fence, hedge"

definition.origin: Possibly from PIE *duh₂-nós "lasting, durable" from *dewh₂- "to last"

definition.meaning: Fort, fortress, stronghold, fortified place; castle
definition.origin: From Old Irish dún, from Proto-Celtic *dūnom
definition.note: Same Celtic root that was borrowed into Germanic as *tūną (town). Cognate pair: Irish dún = English town
Common in place names: Edinburgh (Dùn Èideann), Dundee, Dunbar

definition.meaning: din: fortress, fortified place, camp; dinas: city, fortified town
Fortified place (in place names: Lugdunum=Lyon, Singidunum=Belgrade)
città: city (from Latin civitas); borgo: town, village, borough (from Germanic *burg)
